reasoning_content: We need to determine if 439 is prime. Check divisibility by small primes: 2: no, odd. 3: sum digits 4+3+9=16, not divisible by 3. 5: doesn't end in 0 or 5. 7: 7*62=434, remainder 5, so not divisible. 11: alternating sum 4-3+9=10, not multiple of 11. 13: 13*33=429, remainder 10; 13*34=442 too high. 17: 17*25=425, remainder 14; 17*26=442 too high. 19: 19*23=437, remainder 2; 19*24=456 too high. 23: 23*19=437, remainder 2; 23*20=460. 29: 29*15=435, remainder 4; 29*16=464. 31: 31*14=434, remainder 5; 31*15=465. 37: 37*11=407, remainder 32; 37*12=444 too high. 41: 41*10=410, remainder 29; 41*11=451. 43: 43*10=430, remainder 9; 43*11=473. Next prime sqrt(439) ≈ 20.95, so only need to check primes up to 19 (since 23^2=529>439). Actually sqrt(439) is about 20.95, so primes up to 19 are sufficient. We checked 2,3,5,7,11,13,17,19. None divide. So 439 is prime. Answer yes.
